name: compute_step_output_schema
description: Compute the output schema for a workflow step. This determines what data the step produces. The step parameter must be a valid WorkflowTrigger or WorkflowAction with the correct settings structure for its type.

anyOf.description: Database event trigger that fires when a record is created, updated, deleted, or upserted. The triggered record is accessible in workflow steps via {{trigger.object.fieldName}}.

name.description: Human-readable name for the trigger. Optional but recommended for clarity.

position.description: Position coordinates for the trigger in the workflow diagram. Use (0, 0) for the trigger step.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that the trigger connects to. These are the first steps in the workflow.

eventName.description: Event name in format: objectName.action (e.g., "company.created", "person.updated", "task.deleted", "company.upserted"). Use lowercase object names.

outputSchema.description: Schema defining the output data structure. For database events, this includes the record that triggered the workflow accessible via {{trigger.object.fieldName}}.

anyOf.description: Manual trigger that can be launched by the user. If a record is selected when launched, it is accessible via {{trigger.record.fieldName}}. If no record is selected, no data context is available.

name.description: Human-readable name for the trigger. Optional but recommended for clarity.

position.description: Position coordinates for the trigger in the workflow diagram. Use (0, 0) for the trigger step.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that the trigger connects to. These are the first steps in the workflow.

outputSchema.description: Schema defining the output data structure. When a record is selected, it is accessible via {{trigger.record.fieldName}}. When no record is selected, no data is available.

name.description: Human-readable name for the trigger. Optional but recommended for clarity.

position.description: Position coordinates for the trigger in the workflow diagram. Use (0, 0) for the trigger step.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that the trigger connects to. These are the first steps in the workflow.

name.description: Human-readable name for the trigger. Optional but recommended for clarity.

position.description: Position coordinates for the trigger in the workflow diagram. Use (0, 0) for the trigger step.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that the trigger connects to. These are the first steps in the workflow.

id.description: Unique identifier for the workflow step. Must be unique within the workflow.

name.description: Human-readable name for the workflow step. Should clearly describe what the step does.

valid.description: Whether the step configuration is valid. Set to true when all required fields are properly configured.

nextStepIds.description: Array of step IDs that this step connects to. Leave empty or null for the final step.

position.description: Position coordinates for the step in the workflow diagram.

logicFunctionId.description: The ID of the logic function that holds the code. This is auto-generated when a CODE step is created via create_workflow_version_step — do NOT set this manually.

logicFunctionInput.description: Key-value map of input parameters to pass to the logic function at runtime.

outputSchema.description: Schema defining the output data structure. This data can be referenced in subsequent steps using {{stepId.fieldName}}.

workflowVersionId.description: The UUID of the workflow version

value.description: Whether to retry the action if it fails.
Whether to continue to the next step if this action fails.
